confirmed_issues.description: tick_volume field in candle objects is not validated for non-negative constraint. The plugin stores (string) $candle['volume'] or '0', but a malformed payload with a negative integer volume would be stored as-is in the database.
confirmed_issues.root_cause: No min(0) guard or rejection on candle volume before DB insert in insert_mt5_candle(). Volume is display-only but should satisfy data integrity constraints.
confirmed_issues.proposed_fix: Add max(0, (int) $candle['volume']) guard with an audit event when negative volume is detected. Clamp to 0 rather than reject (volume is display-only and does not affect signal computation).

confirmed_issues.description: npm run lint reports 85 pre-existing Prettier formatting violations (77 errors, 8 warnings) across TypeScript/JSX source files. These exist before this session and are not regression-introduced.
confirmed_issues.root_cause: Prettier formatting was never auto-applied to all src/ files after prettier rules were tightened. The main file with violations is src/routes/plan.tsx.
confirmed_issues.proposed_fix: Run npx prettier --write src/ to auto-fix formatting without any logic changes. No functional code changes.
files_affected: src/routes/plan.tsx and other src/ files (formatting only)

confirmed_issues.description: The workflow spec documents EA payload using quote_time, server_time, and candles[] (array). The LIVE implementation uses timestamp (singular), candle{} (singular M1 object), and candle_m15{} (singular M15 object). Both the EA sender (MQL5) and PHP handler are consistent — only the spec document diverges.
confirmed_issues.root_cause: The spec payload example is illustrative and predates the actual implementation contract. Already documented in phase-0-mt5-ea-market-stream-parity-2026-05-12.md.
confirmed_issues.proposed_fix: Update new parity audit to clearly note this divergence as DOCUMENTED and INTENTIONAL.

confirmed_issues.description: Spread is computed as (ask - bid) / pip_size (float, e.g. 1.3 pips) but the DB column is INT and the format specifier is %d, so fractional pips are truncated (1.3 → 1).
confirmed_issues.root_cause: Schema design choice: spread column is INT, spread is display-only. Fractional pip loss is accepted.
impact_zones: Display only — no signal generation impact
confirmed_issues.proposed_fix: KNOWN LIMITATION — no code change required. Schema uses INT by design for simplicity.

ea_auth_permission_callback.findings: X-EA-API-Key header aliases all correct, hash_equals() used, missing/invalid/unconfigured all handled, user_id validated, wp_set_current_user() called before returning true.

ea_payload_validation.findings: symbol required and validated, timestamp staleness hard-reject at 300s, bid/ask isfinite+positive+bid<=ask, OHLC high>=max(open,close) and low<=min(open,close), candle epoch guard (pre-2000 rejected), candle staleness guard (180s M1, 1800s M15). Missing: volume non-negative guard.

signal_engine_gating.findings: Engine gates on: source=mt5, state=live, age<=staleThresholdSec. Chop >= 0.7 blocks gate. Minimum 30 M15 candles required. Signal persisted with ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE deduplication.

freshness_computation.findings: age_sec computed from broker-supplied updated_at timestamp via iso_age_sec(). NOT from fetch time. FreshnessBadge reads backend state field — does not derive live status client-side.

authority_diagnostics_protection.findings: Route uses permission_user() which requires WP login + read capability. Returns 401 for unauthenticated requests as expected.

admin_routes_protection.findings: All /admin/* routes use permission_admin() requiring manage_options capability.

frontend_api_client.findings: call() function attaches Authorization or X-WP-Nonce headers. cacheBust parameter adds timestamp for GET snapshot. Never fakes live state. Errors are rethrown not swallowed.

streaming_ticks.findings: useStreamingTicks() is purely cosmetic animation between backend ticks. Final sub-tick always snaps to exact backend target value. No live-state is derived from frontend.

parity_mql5_vs_php.findings: All payload fields match between MQL5 sender and PHP receiver. Timestamp UTC handling correct. OHLC validated by both sides. Fib computation is backend-authoritative. No MQL5 fib logic to compare against.
